name: Animal Person
effect: All Charm and Awareness Tests gain two Advantages when dealing with animals friendly to humans and a single Advantage otherwise.
description: You understand and communicate with animals exceptionally well. This can be a natural talent, sheer passionate love for them, or mycoware to improve your senses of smell, body language, and perhaps even manipulate the chemicals needed to communicate with them yourself.

name: Adaptable Eyes
effect: You can see in the dark just as well as in the light.
description: Your eyes have been modified, either with a cybernetic nightvision mode or genetically engineered eyes that mimic the darkvision of many animals. This lets you comfortably keep track of your surroundings even in the total darkness of a moonless night.

name: Assassin
effect: If your Initiative is higher than that of all Enemies or you have taken them by Surprise, the first Combat Test you make during a Match automatically gets a result of 10.
description: You prefer not to fight fair. You know how to strike fast, hard, and preferably by surprise. It is terrifyingly effective.

name: Better Lucky than Good
effect: Any time you roll a 1 when using a Skill you are trained in, you may treat it as if you had rolled a 10 instead.
description: You tend to make your apparent mistakes turn into successes, or perhaps tend to make your successes look like mistakes at first glance. You slip and fall to dodge bullets, drop a bomb about to go off in a way that disarms it, and make faux pas that others find amusing, brave, or endearing.

name: Common Sense
effect: Once per Episode the GM may discreetly inform you that you are about to act on an idea that is not very well thought-out, or give you a clue as to where you should go next if you are stumped for ideas.
description: You are fortunate enough to possess that which is the most uncommon of all senses. You question things that other people take for granted and make well-grounded decisions thinking with your feet firmly on the ground.

name: Danger Instinct
effect: You gain an Advantage to Awareness Tests made to Contest someone else's use of the Deception or Stealth Skills.
description: A combination of gut instinct, attention to detail, and healthy levels of paranoia help you stay in one piece.

name: Deceptive Fighter
effect: As an Action you may Test Charm and Deception against the Defense of one Enemy. If you pass, the target suffers a Disadvantage to all Tests they make and you grant an Advantage to all Offensive Tests made against them until the beginning of your next Turn. For each multiple of 10 you meet with the result of this Test, the number of their Disadvantages and your Advantages each increases by one, or you add another target to suffer the effects.
description: Rather than directly fight, you are at your best performing feints and playing tricks on your enemies to confuse them and leave them open to attacks from your allies.

name: Entertainer
effect: You gain an Advantage to Craftsmanship, Humanities, or Presence Tests involving your chosen field of art.
description: You are an artist at heart. Your talent conveys the depths of the human experience, making others feel joy, sadness, fear, and even anger at your whim.

name: Gamer
effect: This Trait gives you an Advantage to all Tests related to your competitive hobby of choice.
description: You are an exceptionally good player of a specific kind of game and probably have a reputation as the best where you come from.

name: Gumshoe
effect: Once per Episode you may turn a failed Humanities, Investigation, or Sciences Test into a success.
description: You always find any information that you need — or at least enough clues to point you in the right direction. Maybe you don't get the answer right out of the gate, but you always get something to work with.

name: Ingenious
effect: Once per Episode you may gain two Advantages to the use of a Skill that is being applied in a way that is inventive or off the wall.
description: Coming up with new solutions to old problems is its own brand of fun to you. Your ideas might not be the most reliable or even all that good, but no one can accuse you of being unoriginal or unhelpful.

name: Intimidating
effect: You gain an Advantage to all uses of Diplomacy or Deception to make people afraid of you.
description: You can be very scary when you try. Your body language makes others seem smaller, the tone of your voice sends chills down their spines, and looking into your eyes makes people wish they didn't.

name: Jaded
effect: Once per Episode you may turn a failed Willpower Test made to resist being frightened, revulsed, or psychically assaulted into a success.
description: You are largely desensitized to things that others would find shocking, revolting, or worse. The things you've gone through might have even made you cynical or given you a nihilistic outlook on life.

name: Make Do
effect: You do not suffer Disadvantages to Tests when using improper tools or no tools at all.
description: You are used to working without all that fancy expensive equipment and can make the best of what you've got, and are better suited to improvising than most.

name: Polyglot
effect: You understand all spoken and written Languages except for ones that are supposedly dead or alien.
description: Even in a world where most speak English, being able to read and speak multiple languages can be a precious skill. Your linguistic talent is in demand for frontier explorers looking for other civilizations and cultures.

name: Skill Synergy
effect: Once per Episode you may increase the result of a Skill Test made using a Skill you're trained in by an amount equal to 1 per each 10 CP that you've spent on Skills. This can be done after the dice has been rolled.
description: You know how to combine your expertise in various fields of study to get the most out of any of them. You could apply your botany knowledge to the medicinal uses of herbs, your dance skills to combat maneuvers, or your knowledge of vehicular engineering to architecture.

name: Sphere of Influence
effect: You gain an Advantage to all Charm and Resources Tests made to pull strings or deal with people of the chosen field, background, or belonging to a specific organization.
description: You are famous, or perhaps infamous, with a group of people in a way that gives you social capital with them. Maybe they love you, maybe they fear you. What matters is your influence on them.

name: Superior Immune System
effect: Once per Episode you may turn a failed Willpower Test made to resist venoms, diseases, or drugs into a success.
description: Genetic engineering has made you stronger and more resilient to malign toxins. You don't remember when you last caught a cold and you're pretty sure you've never had a hangover.

name: Team Player
effect: You grant an additional Advantage when you use a Help Test to assist an Ally with a Skill you are trained in.
description: There is no "I" in Team and you understand this better than most. Your talent at making others look good means that others love to have you around.

name: Weapon Expertise
effect: You gain an Advantage to Tests relating to knowledge or use of the chosen weapon type.
description: There is a single category of personal-scale weapons that you are a guru in, and you know everything there is to know about their craft, maintenance, use, and history.

name: Ace in the Hole
effect: Once per Episode you may choose one of your Traits that may only be used once per Episode and use it again.
description: You're a tricky one. Your repertoire of ploys is so large that it is effectively impossible to try and predict what you'll do next. Every time someone takes you as finally cornered, you always pull off something new.

name: Attractive
effect: You gain an Advantage to all Tests using Diplomacy, Deception, or Presence with someone who could be attracted to your character.
description: Good looks, a magnetic personality, and mycoware to release special seductive pheromones. These and more can be the weapons in your arsenal that let you influence and manipulate others.

name: Comrade
effect: You have a friendly NPC who occasionally comes to your aid. Once per Episode you may reroll a failed Resources Test, gaining an Advantage to the Test and keeping the new result.
description: While nearly everyone has many people they can rely on, you have an unbreakable bond with a recurring, named NPC ally that is always eager (but not necessarily always available) to help you out.

name: Enhanced Human
effect: Choose an Anomaly when you take this Trait. Once per Episode you may gain the benefits of that Anomaly for the duration of the current Scene. If you do, you also suffer its downsides until the end of the current Episode.
description: Your body is augmented to possess some of the traits that mycotypal humans have. But because you weren't given the symbionts as an embryo your talents are more limited than those of the genuine article.
conditions: Not compatible with Alien Anomaly Traits.

name: Genius
effect: You may substitute any other Attribute with Intellect for the purposes of a Test, and if successful you may not use this Trait again during the same Episode.
description: There is no limit to what your cunning is capable of. Through inventiveness and careful analysis, you can quickly find novel approaches to old tasks, allowing you to get the most done with the least amount of effort.

name: Hard Work and Guts
effect: Once per Episode you may choose to ignore any and all Disadvantages you would suffer and instead transform them into Advantages for a single Test.
description: You are at your best under the worst pressure and have an admirable ability to focus. In your mind, complications just make a challenge much more worth doing.

name: Living Database
effect: If your character could reasonably have been exposed to some factoid or experienced something in the past that you as the Player don't know about or remember yourself, the GM should refresh your memory about it.
description: Your memory is a thing of wonder that has been further cultivated by extensive learning. Obscure names, dates, and other minutiae rarely escape you and you are able to recall events with near-photographic detail.

name: I was Here all Along
effect: Once per Episode you may Test either Stealth or Deception against a DN of 15 to reveal that you were present in a Scene that did not include you. You were either in hiding or disguised as an unassuming background character as appropriate to the Test in question.
description: You are sneaky, everywhere yet nowhere, and generally in places someone else wishes you rather weren't.

name: Jack of all Trades
effect: Once per Episode you may choose a single Skill and gain training in it until the end of the current Scene.
description: You're an exceptionally studious person or someone who went through many different careers. Either way, you know a little bit of everything.

name: Leadership
effect: You may Test Charm and Presence once per Episode to grant up to 5 other Characters an Advantage to all Tests until the end of the current Scene, plus another for each multiple of 10 you meet with the Test. Multiple uses of Leadership by different Characters will not stack — only the highest result will be used.
description: You can inspire and motivate others, even if they barely know you. Whether it is through love or fear, you can draw the best from others so they'll rise to meet any challenge.

name: Plan B
effect: Once per Episode you may reroll a Skill Test made using a Skill you are trained in, gaining an Advantage to the Test and keeping the new result.
description: You are nothing if not well prepared, having failsafes for your contingencies and backups for your auxiliaries. Whenever something important goes wrong, you already know where to start in getting it fixed.

name: Practice Makes Perfect
effect: Any time you roll 4 or less using a Skill you are trained in, you may treat it as if you had rolled a 5 instead.
description: You have a well-practiced, methodical approach to everything that you do that assures you will rarely botch a job. It helps to know a little bit of everything, so you will never be entirely out of your depth.

name: Psychic Power
effect: You reduce all Damage taken from using your Miracle Skills by half.
description: You have attuned your body to maximize efficiency when using psychic powers. You're probably a long-term Glow user, giving your eyes the distinctive glow that the drug is named after.

name: Spirit of Steel
effect: You may substitute any other Attribute with Willpower for the purposes of a Test, and if successful you may not use this Trait again during the same Episode.
description: If you don't succeed at first, try again harder. If it hurts, you get up again and ask for seconds. There is nothing to look forward to but victory, because failure is not an option.

name: The Meat Shield
effect: When Maneuvering during a Match you may share the Defense bonus with one Ally within 1 Zone without having to give up your own Defense bonus.
description: It is a tough job, but someone has to do it. You are one of the best around at protecting those under your care, making you a great bodyguard.

name: Body Suit
effect: You are immune to the harmful effects of vacuums, poison gases, and radiation. The Body Suit will also impair any attempts to physically harm you with a Disadvantage, and grants an Advantage to Willpower Tests made when resisting extreme temperatures.
description: Steel Dragon Industries' Exosuits have kept humans safe from Mechabeasts for decades. While Exosuit teams struggle against the larger and stronger broods, they're much better suited to pest control against the lesser Mechabeast types.

name: Call System
effect: Choose a Superior Proxy you own when you take this Trait. At any time, when you don't have said Equipment on hand, you may have it come to you. Test Resources with a DN of 10; if you succeed the chosen Equipment is delivered to you at the beginning of your next Turn, otherwise it is delivered to you at the end of the current Scene.
description: Your equipment delivers itself to you with the press of a button, following the signal from a specially designed watch.

name: Ether Drive
effect: Choose a Miracle Skill and one Specialization when you take this Trait. You may use said Miracle's Specialization, though without any Advantages to Tests. If this Specialization can make Offensive Tests, you get the bonus Deathblows as well. The Ether Drive will act as a Proxy taking the Damage in your stead.
description: These are experimental Exosuit add-ons from Steel Dragon Industries' R&D. Created from body parts of particularly dangerous Mechabeast types, they can approximate psychic phenomena without any kickback or addictive effects on the user.

name: Masterwork Tool
effect: Choose a General Skill when you take this Trait. All Tests made to perform the chosen Skill with this item on hand are at an Advantage.
description: You are usually considered to have what you need to use your Skills, but it is possible to acquire inherently superior equipment that makes things so much easier.

name: Nanomedical Vest
effect: The first time that you fail a Test to avoid defeat from Plot Armor Damage during an Intermission, you reroll that Test with an Advantage and keep the second result.
description: A form-fitting vest stuffed with medicinal nanobots monitoring your vitals. In case of emergency it delivers anything from first aid to defibrillation.

name: Portable Barrier
effect: Once per Episode, at the beginning of your Turn, you may activate this item to set up a shield that blocks the next 5 Damage you or your Allies may take until the beginning of your next Turn, but only if they remain within their current Zone.
description: A human-sized energy barrier that can be used as cover for a short while before it runs out of power. This item can be made to look like a variety of containers such as a backpack, attache suitcase, or guitar case.

name: Tracking Device
effect: Once per Episode you may make a single Finesse Test against a Character's Defense to place a tracker on them. While it's on the target, the device will tell you how far away the target is and in which direction they are until the target notices the device and removes it at the end of the current Episode.
description: This gadget is small enough to fit in the palm of your hand. You may fire it from an inconspicuous object like a watch or pen and it will attach to the target.

name: Smart Manacles
effect: You may spend an Action to restrain a target that is Defeated, helpless to resist, or willing to put these on. While restrained like this, a character cannot take Actions or perform Tests. At the user's criteria, the target may also be forbidden from speaking. Anybody else can release them using an Action.
description: These shackles automatically adjust to fit the hands of the subject and get more firm the more the prisoner struggles.

name: Smoke Bomb
effect: Once per Episode as an Action you may make a single Contested Stealth against an Investigation Test from everyone who can see you. If you beat everyone else this way, you escape from the Scene and no one can find you for the rest of the Intermission if you don't want to be found.
description: Useful for escaping ninja ambushes and angry significant others alike.

name: Backup Bodies
effect: Once per Episode Arc you may wipe all Damage to your Plot Armor in between Scenes, or devoting a Scene specifically to doing so. If you died, this creates a clone of yourself from before you got hurt.
description: An experimental implementation of the Technocracy brain digitization technology. Test subjects carry within themselves a memory chip that records their digitized personality and genetic information.

name: Flight Pack
effect: Once per Episode you can fly with complete freedom of movement until the end of the current Scene. You fly at the same speed you can move on the ground and while airborne you are immune to attacks that aren't Miracles, Deathblows, using a Range Booster, or coming from characters without Flight Packs of their own.
description: A winged jetpack that lets you move in three dimensions without requiring a running start or the favor of the winds. Many Exosuits have these installed.

name: Miniature Chemical Weapon
effect: Once per Episode and as an Action, you fill a Zone with a toxic substance. Everybody within the Zone Tests Willpower against a DN of 20. Should they fail the Test, they take the amount they failed it by as Damage. If they don't escape the Zone before a Round passes, they will have to Test again. You can throw this and immediately change Zones in the same Action. The toxic substance lasts until the end of the current Scene.
description: One of the most dangerous and illegal things a character could carry on themselves. These miniaturized and super-concentrated breakable orbs pack enough nerve gas to kill an entire apartment room in seconds.

name: Performance Enhancer
effect: Once per Episode and at the beginning of your Turn you may activate this item to gain an Advantage to all Fitness, Awareness, or Willpower Tests for the rest of the Scene.
description: You carry a number of stimulants with you in the form of a medicinal patch that can be applied to your skin in a pinch. With them, you feel like you can do nearly anything. Use with restraint — they are addictive.

name: Personal Facility
effect: Choose two General Skills when you take this Trait. For as long as you are within this Facility, any Tests related to the task it was designed to facilitate involving said Skills gain two Advantages.
description: Your responsibilities afford you a working space that makes your job considerably easier. You are free to design what this facility is for, its equipment, and if it has any staff. The facility is shielded from the interference of G-Reactors.
specializations: Library with Pre-Collapse Books (Humanities and Sciences)

name: Range Booster
effect: Choose a Skill you can use to make Offensive Tests when you take this Trait. You can now make Offensive Tests against targets up to two Zones away from you. If you have another piece of Equipment to boost the corresponding Skill, both are combined into a single item.
description: Exosuits these days often come with medium to long distance weaponry to assist in killing Mechabeast vermin before they so much as scratch a settlement's defense forces.

name: Speed Booster
effect: You can Move two Zones as part of your Actions instead of just one. If you have another piece of Equipment that can Move (either on its own or with someone controlling it), you may combine it with this item.
description: Who doesn't like speed? This item can be made into rocket shoes for yourself or a booster attachment for a vehicle or drone that you own.

name: Self-Destructing Note
effect: Test Electronics to plant the Note on any Electronic device with a screen and camera. After you activate the note's self-destruct function, the first person to read the contents of this note will also be the last — because it explodes in their face. They (and anybody next to them) must Test Willpower against a DN of 10 plus the result of your Electronics Test and take the amount they failed the Test by as Damage.
description: An assassination tool that adds insult to injury — though not necessarily in that order. This is a chip that can be inserted into electronic devices to make the screen show a message of your choice; when someone reads it, it explodes.

name: Superior Proxy
effect: Choose a General Skill when you take this Trait. This Proxy is applicable when using the chosen Skill. A Superior Proxy also grants an Advantage to all Tests using it and imparts a Disadvantage to all Tests trying to damage it.
description: A step above even Masterwork Tools, this equipment not only enhances your performance but also grants protection and takes Damage in your stead.

name: Forbidden Secret Art
effect: Passing the Offensive Test with this Deathblow will destroy that Enemy's current Level of Plot Armor. This happens after the Test deals its regular Damage. The additional Damage is fixed and cannot be reduced or prevented in any way.
description: A favorite of martial artists worldwide. No combat style is complete without a move so lethal it causes foes to collapse just when they thought they'd toughed it out.

name: Sweeping Assault
effect: Your Offensive Test may target all Enemies in the same Zone instead of just one.
description: You attack multiple enemies at once, switching your rifle into the good old spray-and-pray mode, or maybe use an underslung grenade launcher.

name: True Strike
effect: You may choose to reroll the Offensive Test with this Deathblow once, gaining an additional Advantage to the Test and keeping the new result.
description: You are a master of feinting, lulling enemies into a false sense of security before swerving your way into landing a critical hit.

name: Called Shot
effect: Passing the Offensive Test inflicts that Enemy two Disadvantages to all Offensive Tests of their own until the beginning of your next Turn.
description: You disarm, blind, trip up, or otherwise attack your foe in a way that makes it harder for them to attack you back afterwards.

name: Distraction Move
effect: Passing this Offensive Test will grant your Allies an Advantage to all further Offensive Tests against the same Enemy until the beginning of your next Turn.
description: You distract enemies or flush them out of cover with flashbangs, smoke, ricocheting shots, or another similar trick.

name: Finishing Blow
effect: If your Offensive Test would force the Enemy to Test Willpower in order to avoid defeat, they suffer a Disadvantage to said Test for each Layer of Plot Armor they're missing.
description: You strike vital areas with the intent to knock someone out cold or outright kill them in a single blow.

name: Cyborg
effect: You are immune to damage from vacuums, toxins, and illnesses, plus you do not need to eat or sleep — though doing so helps you keep your sanity. You suffer a Disadvantage to the use of Charm with any Test, as well as to any Awareness Test made to contest Diplomacy or Deceit.
description: You're the owner of a body more metal than meat. You could be one of the rare people who prefer cybernetic modifications over mycoware, or one of the rare cyborgs that escaped the Technocracy's conditioning to join the Grid.

name: Nightmare
effect: Once per Episode you may Test Charm and Presence as an Offensive Test against any number of characters that can see or hear you. Any characters that aren't defeated by this Offensive Test will suffer a Disadvantage to all Tests for the rest of the Scene. Any uses of Charm for purposes other than intimidation are at a Disadvantage.
description: Your body has been modified to look particularly intimidating in some way. Your modifications make it difficult to socialize normally, but weaponize your natural charm to paralyze or induce panic in your enemies with a look.

name: Undying
effect: At the end of a Scene in which you took Damage past your first Layer of Plot Armor, you are fully healed back to normal. But for each Layer beyond the first healed this way you suffer a Disadvantage to all Tests for the rest of the Episode.
description: In extremely rare mutations, the mycotypal healing trait is so potent it can regrow missing limbs. The problem is that such regeneration is a notoriously painful and exhausting process.

name: Technobane
effect: Once per Episode you may disable any modern piece of machinery or electronics no larger than yourself in your presence, causing it to malfunction or outright breaking it for the duration of a Scene. All Tests using the Electronics or Vehicles Skills, or those involving Equipment Traits, are at a Disadvantage.
description: You have cybernetic implants to create a miniature and short-lived electromagnetic pulse with which to disrupt modern appliances. Unfortunately, this appears to have an effect even when you're not trying to use it.

name: Wild
effect: You gain an Advantage to all Awareness Tests and uses of the Survival Skill in the wild. You may speak with mundane Earth animals, translating sounds they make into simple and short sentences in your head. This grants you an Advantage to interactions with them. All Tests using Intellect or Resources that don't fit the previously mentioned criteria are at a Disadvantage.
description: You have been genetically engineered with traits taken from other animals rather than fungus-based mycoware. This has made you an excellent survivalist with a natural talent to communicate with animals. The cost is that you struggle to deal with abstract concepts.

name: Augmented Creature
effect: You gain either the Force or Probing Miracle. If you gain it as a Specialist, you suffer a Disadvantage to all Intellect Tests. If you gain it as a Generalist, you suffer two Disadvantages to all Intellect Tests instead. You may purchase the chosen Miracle to buy off the Disadvantages and lose this Trait at any time.
description: You are an uplifted animal that is still getting used to sapience. You often find yourself thinking out loud and are easily confused, sometimes repeating proper nouns in the form of a question.

name: Financially Incompetent
effect: You gain either the Phasing or Portal Miracle. If you gain it as a Specialist, you suffer a Disadvantage to all Resources Tests. If you gain it as a Generalist, you suffer two Disadvantages to all Resources Tests instead. You may purchase the chosen Miracle to buy off the Disadvantages and lose this Trait at any time.
description: There is no concept of money where you come from. You are so used to barter that you are confused by all these abstract numbers that don't mean anything on their own.

name: Hivemind Drone
effect: You gain either the Fortune or Life Miracle. If you gain it as a Specialist, you suffer a Disadvantage to all Willpower Tests. If you gain it as a Generalist, you suffer two Disadvantages to all Willpower Tests instead. You may purchase the chosen Miracle to buy off the Disadvantages and lose this Trait at any time.
description: You used to be part of a greater hivemind as a support unit and find your new sense of individuality rather confusing. Anything that triggers your self-preservation instincts is particularly troublesome to deal with.

name: Mismatched Body
effect: You gain either the Phantasm or Temperature Miracle. If you gain it as a Specialist, you suffer a Disadvantage to all Fitness Tests. If you gain it as a Generalist, you suffer two Disadvantages to all Fitness Tests instead. You may purchase the chosen Miracle to buy off the Disadvantages and lose this Trait at any time.
description: You have recently acquired a human body to better infiltrate their society with — and it sucks. They don't have enough legs, depend almost entirely on their sight, and don't even have a proper exoskeleton to protect their squishy organs!

name: Offbeat Senses
effect: You gain either the Matter or Electricity Miracle. If you gain it as a Specialist, you suffer a Disadvantage to all Awareness Tests. If you gain it as a Generalist, you suffer two Disadvantages to all Awareness Tests instead. You may purchase the chosen Miracle to buy off the Disadvantages and lose this Trait at any time.
description: Excessive Glow consumption has affected your senses to the point that everything is weird. The world is painted in watercolor, people's faces are like blobs of old memories, and sometimes you think you can see time itself.

name: Wrong Language Data
effect: You gain either the Sight or Somatics Miracle. If you gain it as a Specialist, you suffer a Disadvantage to all Charm Tests. If you gain it as a Generalist, you suffer two Disadvantages to all Charm Tests instead. You may purchase the chosen Miracle to buy off the Disadvantages and lose this Trait at any time.
description: Your installation of the local language data has a bug in the form of one or more speech quirks. Perhaps you can only speak in the third person or in archaic Olde English. While a few find it endearing, most can't take you seriously.
